$generated: authored at P4 from the P0 catalog — reviewed at P4.5 (R9)
$policy: freeze-and-diverge (§10.5): a point-in-time extraction of the donor squad's two hardened `--settings` profiles. Donor evolution is NOT auto-backported.

$install.$doc: Each profile is written to its {{SETTINGS_PROFILE_PATHS}} entry (account hole; minted at P5, lives OUTSIDE the repo so a `git revert` cannot restore it) and referenced by the consuming seat's `--settings` custom-arg. BEFORE launching a seat, assert its target exists AND parses as JSON — a missing/corrupt target would silently launch the seat with an empty-deny profile. GUARDRAIL, NOT A CAGE: the binding rule is the constitution boundary; these profiles are defense-in-depth.

$note: deny-only posture (the donor profiles carry no allow-list). The credential/exec/egress denylists are the portable, battle-tested baseline — denying a path or command that does not exist is a harmless no-op, so P0 may EXTEND them with any extra runtime credential dirs the target account uses. Only donor-absolute paths are holed; the tilde-relative credential paths and the generic command tokens ship as STATIC defaults.

hardened.$doc: code-probing seat (the Validator, Phase 1.5). Full credential read-deny + full exec/egress lockdown so a member-authorized code probe cannot read a secret or open a network channel. Pairs with the hardened-restricted MCP profile (exa-egress-restriction).
hardened.consumed_by: the seat whose mcp-asserts include exa-egress-restriction (the Validator row)

restricted.$doc: claude-runtime curator seat (the Mentor). Disables ambient plugins so the seat runs lean on ONLY its explicit `--mcp-config` servers, and denies reads of credential/tooling paths plus the profile file itself. The memory backend rides the seat's `--mcp-config`, so its plugin is disabled here too.
restricted.consumed_by: the curator seat on the claude runtime (memory-mounted, no code-probe grant)

enabledPlugins.$generated-from: the ambient plugin set probed at P0 (the same preflight that resolves {{MCP_SERVER_CATALOG}})
enabledPlugins.$rule: emit one "<plugin-id>": false entry per installed ambient plugin — a restricted seat disables ALL of them and runs on only its explicit `--mcp-config` servers (the memory backend included). Account-coupled, so no plugin ids are baked here. do not hand-edit — regenerated at P4/P7.
